TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan tsug a marivu katua vaɫaw.
ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan cug a marivu katua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan liked to quarrel with his wife.

S.FORM: nu keman ini pakani, marka veɫatjen.
nu keman ini pakani, marka veljatjen.
S.TRANSL: When they ate he didn’t give her food, but they hit one another(?).

S.FORM: qau limutseng azua vaɫaw; vaik a ɫetaladj tua verung nua tsuqu.
qau limuceng azua valjaw; vaik a ljetaladj tua verung nua cuqu.
S.TRANSL: His wife was angry, and went into a hole in a bischoffia tree.

S.FORM: qau tsuatsuay anga malap ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan tua putjaw, sa putjavi a tsuqu, a muri paɫekatsauan tua vaɫaw.
qau cuacuay anga malap ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan tua putjaw, sa putjavi a cuqu, a muri paljekacauan tua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: After a time Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan took an axe and chopped at the tree to get his wife out.

S.FORM: manu "teng," aya, milayap a sema tjai likuz tua ɫavek.
manu "teng," aya, milayap a sema tjai likuz tua ljavek.
S.TRANSL: But it (or she) said “Tung,” and she flew to the far side of the sea.

S.FORM: tsuatsuay anga mapuvarung ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
cuacuay anga mapuvarung ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: After a time Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan got anxious.

S.FORM: qemaqivu tua sikataqaɫan tu vaik a qemaɫup.
qemaqivu tua sikataqaljan tu vaik a qemaljup.
S.TRANSL: He called his fellow-villagers to go hunting.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan: "tiaken makamaza ken i dungdung tua ɫavek.
qau kiljavaran ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan: "tiaken makamaza ken i dungdung tua ljavek.
S.TRANSL: So Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan said: “I’ll go along here near the sea.

S.FORM: timun pakazua-u itjua pungayan."
timun pakazua-u itjua pungayan."
S.TRANSL: You go along by the cliffs.”

S.FORM: manu neka nu satsemel.
manu neka nu sacemel.
S.TRANSL: There was no wild game.

S.FORM: nu paluku, qatjuvi marka ngaḍi sakamaya.
nu paluku, qatjuvi marka ngadri sakamaya.
S.TRANSL: If they started anything, it was just snakes and badgers.

S.FORM: qau kirivu a sikataqaɫan.
qau kirivu a sikataqaljan.
S.TRANSL: The villagers complained:

S.FORM: "aku neka nu satsemel?" aya kivadaq tjai sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
"aku neka nu sacemel?" aya kivadaq tjai sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: “Why is there no game?” they asked Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan: "nuka qatjuvi, nuka ngaḍi kuangu," aya kiɫavaran.
qau kiljavaran ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan: "nuka qatjuvi, nuka ngadri kuangu," aya kiljavaran.
S.TRANSL: He replied: “Shoot even snakes and badgers,” he said.

S.FORM: qau timadju izua i ɫavek a qarawqawqaw sakamaya.
qau timadju izua i ljavek a qarawqawqaw sakamaya.
S.TRANSL: And he was there by the sea shouting away.

S.FORM: qau a sikataqaɫan: "anema kudakudain nazua tsautsau?" aya kinemnem.
qau a sikataqaljan: "anema kudakudain nazua caucau?" aya kinemnem.
S.TRANSL: The villagers thought: “What’s this fellow up to?”

S.FORM: qau vaiken a kiqenetj.
qau vaiken a kiqenetj.
S.TRANSL: They went to have a look.

S.FORM: mintu "ɫauɫauɫavu a djaraɫap," ayaya semenasenay.
mintu "ljauljauljavu a djaraljap," ayaya semenasenay.
S.TRANSL: And he was singing: “Stretch out, banyan tree!”.

S.FORM: qau tsuay anga ɫemauɫau a djaraɫap a sema tjai likuz tua ɫavek.
qau cuay anga ljemauljau a djaraljap a sema tjai likuz tua ljavek.
S.TRANSL: After a time the tree stretched out to the far side of the sea.

S.FORM: qau azua ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan semusu a vaik tua djaraɫap.
qau azua ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan semusu a vaik tua djaraljap.
S.TRANSL: And Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan went along the tree.

S.FORM: mintu tsaluman tua qinaɫivan nua tapaw nua vaɫaw.
mintu caluman tua qinaljivan nua tapaw nua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: He reached the roof of his wife’s house,

S.FORM: qau sualapen a itung, sa vuɫuqan a paɫeteku.
qau sualapen a itung, sa vuljuqan a paljeteku.
S.TRANSL: and took off his clothes and threw them down below.

S.FORM: manu djumaken nua aɫak nua vaɫaw.
manu djumaken nua aljak nua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: His wife’s child found them.

S.FORM: "aku uzaitsu a itung, kina?
"aku uzaicu a itung, kina?
S.TRANSL: “Why are these clothes here, mother?

S.FORM: matu su inalangan."
matu su inalangan."
S.TRANSL: They look like ones made by you.”

S.FORM: "maka inu ti tjama tua ɫavek?"
"maka inu ti tjama tua ljavek?"
S.TRANSL: “How did your father get here across the sea?”

S.FORM: qau sualapen ni sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan a dangiɫan, sa vuɫuqan uta.
qau sualapen ni sa Puljaljuyaljuyan a dangiljan, sa vuljuqan uta.
S.TRANSL: Then Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan took off his apron (loincloth?) and threw that down too.

S.FORM: qau djumaken nua kakeḍian
qau djumaken nua kakedrian
S.TRANSL: The child found it.

S.FORM: "aku uzaitsu a dangiɫan?
"aku uzaicu a dangiljan?
S.TRANSL: “Why is this apron here?

S.FORM: matu su inalangan."
matu su inalangan."
S.TRANSL: It looks like one made by you.”

S.FORM: qau ɫetsasaw a kina, sa kiqenetji a pasa qinaɫivan.
qau ljecasaw a kina, sa kiqenetji a pasa qinaljivan.
S.TRANSL: The mother went out, and looked up at the roof.

S.FORM: mintu djemelidjeli ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan i vavaw tua qinaɫivan.
mintu djemelidjeli ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan i vavaw tua qinaljivan.
S.TRANSL: There was Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan laughing on the roof.

S.FORM: qaqivuin anga nua vaɫaw a pasa qumaqan.
qaqivuin anga nua valjaw a pasa qumaqan.
S.TRANSL: His wife called to him to come indoors.

S.FORM: qau maseleman anga, keman tua kaiven.
qau maseleman anga, keman tua kaiven.
S.TRANSL: After dark they sat down to supper.

S.FORM: manu ini kan ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
manu ini kan ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan didn’t eat.

S.FORM: qau kivadaqan nua vaɫaw: "anema su tjenglayan a keman?" ayain.
qau kivadaqan nua valjaw: "anema su tjenglayan a keman?" ayain.
S.TRANSL: His wife asked him: “What would you like to eat?”

S.FORM: "avan a vaɫetjuk sakamaya a tjenglayan a keman."
"avan a valjetjuk sakamaya a tjenglayan a keman."
S.TRANSL: “I would just like to eat an orange (or oranges).”

S.FORM: qau siyaya tua vaɫetjuk sa pakani.
qau siyaya tua valjetjuk sa pakani.
S.TRANSL: So she picked an orange for him and gave it to him to eat.

S.FORM: qau muri qemereng anga.
qau muri qemereng anga.
S.TRANSL: When they were going to lie down,

S.FORM: kiɫavaran ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan: "qemereng aken i kulakula," aya kiɫavaran tua vaɫaw.
kiljavaran ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan: "qemereng aken i kulakula," aya kiljavaran tua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan said: “I’ll lie at the foot end,” he said to his wife.

S.FORM: a vaɫaw: "maya qemereng i kulakula; makulakula sun."
a valjaw: "maya qemereng i kulakula; makulakula sun."
S.TRANSL: His wife said: “Don’t lie at the foot end; you’ll have trouble with the feet.”

S.FORM: "qemereng aken i ɫaving," aya ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
"qemereng aken i ljaving," aya ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: “I’ll lie at the edge,” said Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.

S.FORM: kiɫavaran a vaɫaw: "maya qemereng i ɫaviɫaving; maɫaviɫaving sun.
kiljavaran a valjaw: "maya qemereng i ljaviljaving; maljaviljaving sun.
S.TRANSL: His wife said: “Don’t lie at the side; you’ll have trouble at the edge.

S.FORM: qerengu izua itjua qeluz," aya a vaɫaw.
qerengu izua itjua qeluz," aya a valjaw.
S.TRANSL: Lie there by the pillar,” said his wife.

S.FORM: qau vaik a qemereng sema tjua qeluz ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
qau vaik a qemereng sema tjua qeluz ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: So Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan went to lie by the pillar.

S.FORM: ita vengin inika taqed, qaritjqitjqitj.
ita vengin inika taqed, qaritjqitjqitj.
S.TRANSL: The whole night he didn’t sleep; he was just grinding his teeth.

S.FORM: kivadaqan nua vaɫaw ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan: "makuda sun?
kivadaqan nua valjaw ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan: "makuda sun?
S.TRANSL: His wife asked him: “What’s the matter with you?

S.FORM: aku ini sun a taqed?
aku ini sun a taqed?
S.TRANSL: Why don’t you sleep?

S.FORM: aku qaritjqitjqitj sun?"
aku qaritjqitjqitj sun?"
S.TRANSL: Why do you grind your teeth?”

S.FORM: "maitazuangata nu keman aken tua vaɫetjuk," aya ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan.
"maitazuangata nu keman aken tua valjetjuk," aya ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan.
S.TRANSL: “It’s always just like that when I eat oranges,” he said.

S.FORM: manu pentjupetjuq tua qeluz nua tapaw.
manu pentjupetjuq tua qeluz nua tapaw.
S.TRANSL: In fact he was pulling out the pillar of the house.

S.FORM: qau sa pasusu-i tua naɫemauɫaw a djaraɫap a tapaw.
qau sa pasusu-i tua naljemauljaw a djaraljap a tapaw.
S.TRANSL: And he caused the whole house to follow the line of the stretched out banyan tree.

S.FORM: qau maɫianga patseged anga tiamadju a mapuɫat.
qau maljianga paceged anga tiamadju a mapuljat.
S.TRANSL: Next day they all woke up.

S.FORM: mintu i ɫaviɫaving anga tua ɫavek.
mintu i ljaviljaving anga tua ljavek.
S.TRANSL: And there they were on the edge of the sea.

S.FORM: qau mingtjus anga azua vaɫaw.
qau mingtjus anga azua valjaw.
S.TRANSL: His wife was startled.

S.FORM: "aku imazanga ken?" aya kiɫavaran.
"aku imazanga ken?" aya kiljavaran.
S.TRANSL: “Why am I here now?” she said.

S.FORM: qau kiɫavaran ti sa Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan: "tiaken a nakematsu a pasa maza.
qau kiljavaran ti sa Puljaljuyaljuyan: "tiaken a nakemacu a pasa maza.
S.TRANSL: Puɫaɫuyaɫuyan said: “It was I who brought you here.

S.FORM: ini anga nakuya aken.
ini anga nakuya aken.
S.TRANSL: I’m not bad any more.

S.FORM: mayanga mavilad.
mayanga mavilad.
S.TRANSL: Don’t run away.

S.FORM: a pareka nanguanguaqi anga itjen mapuɫat."
a pareka nanguanguaqi anga itjen mapuljat."
S.TRANSL: Let’s all be good to one another.”
